mission.title: kyverno: [Feature] Extend `foreach` to cover generate rules
mission.description: [Feature] Extend `foreach` to cover generate rules. Requested by 12+ users.

steps.title: Check current kyverno deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kyverno version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
helm list -n kyverno 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kyverno installation.

steps.title: Review kyverno configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kyverno configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
kubectl get configmap -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kyverno
```
### Problem Statement

The `foreach` ability covers validate and mutate rules, but not generate.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Feature] Extend `foreach` to cover generate rules
steps.description:
Extends foreach to cover generate rules.
Done 👍🏽 

### Proof Manifests

If the pollicy given below is applied then multiple configMap will get created on the creation of a pod:

```yaml
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: basic-policy2
spec:
  rules:
  - name:
```yaml
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: basic-policy2
spec:
  rules:
  - name: test-foreach-generate
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    generate:
      foreach:
      - list: "request.object.spec.containers"
        kind: ConfigMap
        apiVersion: v1
        name: "custom-created-configmap"
        namespace: "{{request.object.metadata.namespace}}"
        synchronize: false
        data:
          data:
            foo: "bar is my container name"
      - list: "request.object.spec.containers"
        kind: ConfigMap
      
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
kubectl get events -n kyverno --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[Feature] Extend `foreach` to cover generate rules" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Extends foreach to cover generate rules.
Done 👍🏽 

### Proof Manifests

If the pollicy given below is applied then multiple configMap will get created on the creation of a pod:

apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:

spec:
  rules:
  - name: test-foreach-generate
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kyverno installed or the issue environment reproducible.
